Disappointed with my recent experience with @Nike 👟

Bought a pair of running shoes, wore them once for a short 1-mile run to properly test them — and was told they cannot be returned because they were “used”.

What was more frustrating was the customer service experience. No name. No personal touch. Just a copy-and-paste reference to policy. No attempt to understand the situation. No ownership. Just policy.

For a premium global brand, that feels impersonal and transactional.

What surprises me most is that brands like @BrooksRunning, @ASICS and @HOKA are confident enough in their product quality to allow customers to actually try their shoes. They prioritise comfort and fit — because running shoes can’t truly be tested walking around your living room.

When you’re paying premium prices, you expect premium service.

This isn’t about breaking rules — it’s about how customers are treated.

Loyalty is built on experience, not policy.

#Nike #CustomerExperience #RunningCommunity #RunnerLife #Sportswear #Disappointed #CustomerService #BrandLoyalty #RunningShoes #UKRunners

Disappointed with my recent experience with @Nike 👟

Bought a pair of running shoes, wore them once for a short 1-mile run to properly test them — and was told they cannot be returned because they were “used”.

What was more frustrating was the customer service experience. No name. No personal touch. Just a copy-and-paste reference to policy. No attempt to understand the situation. No ownership. Just policy.

For a premium global brand, that feels impersonal and transactional.

What surprises me most is that brands like @BrooksRunning, @ASICS and @HOKA are confident enough in their product quality to allow customers to actually try their shoes. They prioritise comfort and fit — because running shoes can’t truly be tested walking around your living room.

When you’re paying premium prices, you expect premium service.

This isn’t about breaking rules — it’s about how customers are treated.

Loyalty is built on experience, not policy.

#Nike #CustomerExperience #RunningCommunity #RunnerLife #Sportswear #Disappointed #CustomerService #BrandLoyalty #RunningShoes #UKRunners
